description: Agilent E4438C ESG Vector Signal Generator (250 kHz to 6 GHz, frequency range option-dependent: Opt 501=1 GHz, 502=2 GHz, 503=3 GHz, 504=4 GHz, 506=6 GHz). SCPI-controlled over GPIB and LAN. Provides analog modulation (AM/FM/PhiM, pulse), I/Q modulation, an internal dual arbitrary waveform (ARB) baseband generator, real-time digital modulation (Custom/TDMA), and optional format personalities (GSM, EDGE, W-CDMA, cdma2000, DECT, PHS, PDC, TETRa, GPS, etc.). Supports CW, step/list frequency and power sweeps. This definition covers the core programmable control surface (frequency, power/amplitude, output, analog and digital modulation, ARB/real-time, list/sweep, reference oscillator, trigger, memory/mass-memory waveform handling, display, system, status, and common * commands); the thousands of per-personality leaf commands are not exhaustively enumerated.

identity.description: Returns manufacturer, model (E4438C), serial number, and firmware revision.

commands.name: idn_query
commands.description: Returns the instrument identification string: manufacturer, model number, serial number, firmware revision.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Returns the signal generator to a defined instrument state (factory-defined reset state).

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clears the Status Byte register, the data questionable and standard event status event registers, and the error queue.

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: Sets bit 0 in the standard event status register when all pending operations complete. The query form returns 1 when all operations are complete.

commands.name: wait_to_continue
commands.description: Prohibits the instrument from executing any new commands until all pending overlapped commands have completed.

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Sets (or queries) the standard event status enable register.

commands.name: event_status_register
commands.description: Queries and clears the standard event status register.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Sets (or queries) the value of the service request enable register.

commands.name: status_byte_query
commands.description: Queries the status byte register.

commands.name: identify_query_alt
commands.description: Returns a list of the options installed in the signal generator.

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: Restores the signal generator to a state previously stored in the specified register and sequence.

commands.name: save_state
commands.description: Saves the current instrument state to the specified memory register and sequence.

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: Triggers the device if BUS is the selected trigger source, otherwise it is ignored.

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Initiates the internal self-test and returns the result (0 = pass).

commands.name: recall_setup
commands.description: Sets (or queries) the power-on status clear flag, controlling automatic power-on clearing of the enable registers.

commands.name: frequency_cw
commands.description: Sets the signal generator output frequency for CW frequency mode, or increments/decrements the current RF frequency by the step value.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 100 kHz to 6 GHz (option-dependent: 501=1 GHz, 502=2 GHz, 503=3 GHz, 504=4 GHz, 506=6 GHz)

commands.name: frequency_step_increment
commands.description: Sets the step increment used by the FREQuency UP/DOWN command and the front-panel arrow keys.

commands.name: frequency_fixed
commands.description: Sets the signal generator output frequency for the fixed (CW) frequency mode.

commands.name: frequency_mode
commands.description: Sets the frequency mode of the signal generator: continuous wave (CW/FIXed) or list/step sweep (LIST).

commands.name: frequency_start
commands.description: Sets the first frequency point in a step sweep.

commands.name: frequency_stop
commands.description: Sets the last frequency point in a step sweep.

commands.name: frequency_multiplier
commands.description: Sets the multiplier for the displayed frequency, used when an external multiplier follows the source.

commands.name: frequency_offset
commands.description: Sets a frequency offset value applied to the displayed frequency.

commands.name: frequency_offset_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the frequency offset function.

commands.name: frequency_reference
commands.description: Sets the frequency reference value; subsequent frequency settings are relative to this reference when reference mode is on.

commands.name: frequency_reference_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the frequency reference mode.

commands.name: frequency_channels_band
commands.description: Selects the frequency band used for channel-number-based frequency entry.

commands.name: frequency_channels_number
commands.description: Sets the channel number within the selected band when channel mode is on.

commands.name: frequency_channels_state
commands.description: Enables or disables channel-number-based frequency entry.

commands.name: phase_adjust
commands.description: Adjusts the phase of the output signal relative to the phase reference.

commands.name: phase_reference
commands.description: Sets the current output phase as a zero reference.

commands.name: power_amplitude
commands.description: Sets the RF output power level.

parameters.name: amplitude
parameters.range: approx -136 to +17 dBm (option/frequency-dependent; Std up to +13 dBm, Opt UNB up to +17 dBm)

commands.name: power_step_increment
commands.description: Sets the incremental step value for the amplitude parameter, or increments/decrements the current output power.

commands.name: power_offset
commands.description: Sets a power offset value applied to the displayed/output amplitude.

commands.name: power_mode
commands.description: Sets the power mode: fixed amplitude (FIXed) or list/step sweep (LIST).

commands.name: power_start
commands.description: Sets the first amplitude point in a step sweep.

commands.name: power_stop
commands.description: Sets the last amplitude point in a step sweep.

commands.name: power_reference
commands.description: Sets the power reference value; output power settings are relative to this reference when reference mode is on.

commands.name: power_reference_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the power reference mode.

commands.name: power_attenuation
commands.description: Sets the attenuator value (when attenuator auto is off).

commands.name: power_attenuation_auto
commands.description: Enables or disables automatic attenuator switching (attenuator hold off/on).

commands.name: power_alc_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the automatic level control (ALC) loop.

commands.name: power_alc_bandwidth
commands.description: Sets the ALC loop bandwidth.

commands.name: power_alc_bandwidth_auto
commands.description: Enables or disables automatic selection of the ALC loop bandwidth.

commands.name: power_alc_level
commands.description: Sets the ALC level for the modulator attenuator.

commands.name: power_alc_search
commands.description: Enables or disables the internal power search calibration (used when ALC is off), or initiates a single search.

commands.name: output_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the RF output.

commands.name: output_modulation_state
commands.description: Enables or disables all currently active modulation outputs (the front-panel Mod On/Off).

commands.name: output_blanking_state
commands.description: Enables or disables RF output blanking during frequency changes/retrace.

commands.name: output_blanking_auto
commands.description: Enables or disables automatic RF output blanking.

commands.name: am_state
commands.description: Enables or disables amplitude modulation for the selected AM path (1 or 2).

commands.name: am_depth
commands.description: Sets the linear amplitude modulation depth (in percent) for the selected AM path.

commands.name: am_source
commands.description: Selects the AM modulation source (internal generator or external input).

commands.name: am_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets the internal modulation frequency for the AM source.

commands.name: am_internal_function_shape
commands.description: Selects the internal modulation waveform shape for the AM source.

parameters.name: shape
parameters.range: SINE | TRIangle | SQUare | RAMP | NOISe | DUALsine | SWEPtsine

commands.name: am_external_coupling
commands.description: Selects AC or DC coupling for the external AM input.

commands.name: am_wideband_state
commands.description: Enables or disables wideband AM.

commands.name: fm_state
commands.description: Enables or disables frequency modulation for the selected FM path (1 or 2).

commands.name: fm_deviation
commands.description: Sets the frequency modulation peak deviation for the selected FM path.

commands.name: fm_source
commands.description: Selects the FM modulation source (internal generator or external input).

commands.name: fm_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets the internal modulation frequency for the FM source.

commands.name: fm_internal_function_shape
commands.description: Selects the internal modulation waveform shape for the FM source.

parameters.name: shape
parameters.range: SINE | TRIangle | SQUare | RAMP | NOISe | DUALsine | SWEPtsine

commands.name: fm_external_coupling
commands.description: Selects AC or DC coupling for the external FM input.

commands.name: pm_state
commands.description: Enables or disables phase modulation for the selected PhiM path (1 or 2).

commands.name: pm_deviation
commands.description: Sets the phase modulation peak deviation for the selected PhiM path.

commands.name: pm_source
commands.description: Selects the PhiM modulation source (internal generator or external input).

commands.name: pm_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets the internal modulation frequency for the PhiM source.

commands.name: pm_internal_function_shape
commands.description: Selects the internal modulation waveform shape for the PhiM source.

parameters.name: shape
parameters.range: SINE | TRIangle | SQUare | RAMP | NOISe | DUALsine | SWEPtsine

commands.name: pm_bandwidth
commands.description: Selects normal or high bandwidth mode for phase modulation.

commands.name: pulm_state
commands.description: Enables or disables pulse modulation.

commands.name: pulm_source
commands.description: Selects the pulse modulation source: internal square/pulse generator or external input.

commands.name: pulm_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets the internal pulse modulation rate (frequency).

commands.name: pulm_internal_period
commands.description: Sets the period of the internal pulse modulation generator.

commands.name: pulm_internal_pwidth
commands.description: Sets the pulse width of the internal pulse modulation generator.

commands.name: pulm_internal_function_shape
commands.description: Selects the internal pulse generator waveform shape (square or pulse).

commands.name: lfoutput_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the low-frequency (LF) output.

commands.name: lfoutput_amplitude
commands.description: Sets the amplitude of the LF output signal.

commands.name: lfoutput_source
commands.description: Selects the source routed to the LF output (internal function generators or modulation monitor).

commands.name: lfoutput_function_frequency
commands.description: Sets the frequency of the LF output internal function generator.

commands.name: lfoutput_function_shape
commands.description: Selects the waveform shape of the LF output internal function generator.

commands.name: dm_state
commands.description: Enables or disables external I/Q (vector) modulation.

commands.name: dm_source
commands.description: Selects the I/Q modulation source (external input, internal baseband generator, etc.).

parameters.name: source
parameters.range: EXTernal | INTernal | BBG1 | EXT600 | OFF | SUM

commands.name: dm_iqadjustment_state
commands.description: Enables or disables I/Q adjustments (gain, offset, quadrature skew).

commands.name: dm_iqadjustment_gain
commands.description: Sets the I/Q gain balance adjustment.

commands.name: dm_iqadjustment_ioffset
commands.description: Sets the I-channel DC offset adjustment (percent of full scale).

commands.name: dm_iqadjustment_qoffset
commands.description: Sets the Q-channel DC offset adjustment (percent of full scale).

commands.name: dm_iqadjustment_qskew
commands.description: Sets the I/Q quadrature angle (skew) adjustment.

commands.name: dm_polarity
commands.description: Sets the I/Q modulation polarity (normal or inverted).

commands.name: radio_arb_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the dual arbitrary waveform (ARB) baseband generator. Turning on ARB also enables I/Q modulation.

commands.name: radio_arb_waveform
commands.description: Selects a waveform segment or sequence file from waveform memory (WFM1) or sequence (SEQ) for ARB playback.

commands.name: radio_arb_clock_rate
commands.description: Sets the ARB sample (waveform) clock rate.

commands.name: radio_arb_scaling
commands.description: Sets the runtime scaling (in percent) applied to the ARB waveform to avoid DAC overflow.

commands.name: radio_arb_trigger_source
commands.description: Selects the ARB trigger source.

commands.name: radio_arb_trigger_type
commands.description: Selects the ARB trigger type (continuous, single, gated, or segment advance).

commands.name: radio_arb_retrigger
commands.description: Sets the behavior when a trigger is received during waveform playback (on, off, or immediate).

commands.name: radio_arb_reference_source
commands.description: Selects the ARB sample-clock reference source (internal or external).

commands.name: radio_arb_noise_state
commands.description: Enables or disables additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) on the ARB waveform.

commands.name: radio_arb_noise_cn
commands.description: Sets the carrier-to-noise ratio for the ARB noise function.

commands.name: radio_arb_generate_sine
commands.description: Generates an I/Q sine (or sine cal) waveform into ARB waveform memory.

commands.name: radio_arb_filter_type
commands.description: Selects the baseband filter type applied by the ARB.

commands.name: radio_arb_filter_alpha
commands.description: Sets the filter alpha (roll-off) for Nyquist/root-Nyquist filters.

commands.name: radio_arb_sequence
commands.description: Defines a waveform sequence by listing waveform segments, repetitions, and markers.

commands.name: radio_custom_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the real-time Custom (arbitrary single-carrier) digital modulation personality.

commands.name: radio_gsm_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the GSM digital modulation personality.

commands.name: radio_edge_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the EDGE digital modulation personality.

commands.name: radio_dect_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the DECT digital modulation personality.

commands.name: radio_pdc_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the PDC digital modulation personality.

commands.name: radio_phs_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the PHS digital modulation personality.

commands.name: radio_tetra_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the TETRA digital modulation personality.

commands.name: radio_gps_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the GPS personality.

commands.name: list_mode
commands.description: Selects the list mode operation: stepping through a list (LIST) or manual point selection (MANual).

commands.name: list_type
commands.description: Selects the sweep list type: list (LIST) or step (STEP).

commands.name: list_frequency
commands.description: Defines the list of frequency points for list-mode sweep.

commands.name: list_frequency_points
commands.description: Queries the number of frequency points in the list.

commands.name: list_power
commands.description: Defines the list of power (amplitude) points for list-mode sweep.

commands.name: list_power_points
commands.description: Queries the number of power points in the list.

commands.name: list_dwell
commands.description: Defines the list of dwell times (seconds per point) for list-mode sweep.

commands.name: list_dwell_points
commands.description: Queries the number of dwell-time points in the list.

commands.name: list_direction
commands.description: Sets the sweep direction (up or down) for list/step mode.

commands.name: list_manual
commands.description: Selects the point in the list to output when LIST mode is MANual.

commands.name: list_trigger_source
commands.description: Selects the point-to-point trigger source for list/step sweep.

commands.name: list_retrace
commands.description: Sets whether a single sweep retraces (returns to the first point) at completion.

commands.name: list_type_list_initialize_fstep
commands.description: Replaces the loaded list data with the current step-sweep frequency, power, and dwell values.

commands.name: list_type_list_initialize_preset
commands.description: Replaces the loaded list data with a single point at the current frequency, power, and dwell.

commands.name: sweep_dwell
commands.description: Sets the dwell time for step sweep (time spent at each point).

commands.name: sweep_points
commands.description: Sets the number of points in a step sweep.

commands.name: roscillator_source
commands.description: Queries (or under AUTO=OFF, indicates) the reference oscillator source in use (internal or external).

commands.name: roscillator_source_auto
commands.description: Enables or disables automatic selection of the reference oscillator source (auto-detect external reference).

commands.name: unit_power
commands.description: Sets the default amplitude units used for power entry and query.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.range: DBM | DBUV | DBUVEMF | V | VEMF | DB

commands.name: trigger_immediate
commands.description: Sets the trigger to immediate mode (free run) or generates an immediate trigger event for the sweep/list.

commands.name: trigger_source
commands.description: Selects the sweep/list trigger source.

commands.name: trigger_slope
commands.description: Selects the active edge (positive or negative) of the external trigger input.

commands.name: trigger_type
commands.description: Selects the sweep trigger type (e.g. point-to-point or single sweep).

commands.name: trigger_output_polarity
commands.description: Selects the polarity (positive/negative) of the trigger output signal.

commands.name: correction_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the user-flatness corrections.

commands.name: correction_flatness_pair
commands.description: Sets a frequency and amplitude correction pair in the user-flatness array (max 1601 points).

commands.name: correction_flatness_load
commands.description: Loads a user-flatness correction file from the USERFLAT catalog.

commands.name: correction_flatness_store
commands.description: Stores the current user-flatness correction data to a named file.

commands.name: correction_flatness_preset
commands.description: Presets the user-flatness correction to a single factory-defined point. Overwrites current data.

commands.name: correction_flatness_points
commands.description: Queries the number of points in the user-flatness correction file.

commands.name: display_state
commands.description: Turns the front-panel display on or off.

commands.name: display_brightness
commands.description: Sets the display brightness.

commands.name: display_contrast
commands.description: Sets the display contrast.

commands.name: display_inverse
commands.description: Sets the display to inverse (reverse) video.

commands.name: display_remote
commands.description: Enables or disables the larger remote-display update mode.

commands.name: display_annotation_amplitude_unit
commands.description: Selects the amplitude annotation units shown on the display.

commands.name: memory_catalog_all
commands.description: Outputs a catalog (list) of all files in internal memory, or of a specific file type.

commands.name: memory_data
commands.description: Loads or queries binary data (e.g. waveform, FIR, list) into a named file in memory using IEEE 488.2 definite-length block format.

commands.name: memory_data_iq
commands.description: Downloads I/Q waveform data into a named ARB waveform segment in memory.

commands.name: memory_delete_name
commands.description: Deletes a named file from internal memory.

commands.name: memory_delete_all
commands.description: Deletes all files of a given type (or all files) from internal memory.

commands.name: memory_copy_name
commands.description: Copies a file in internal memory to a new name.

commands.name: memory_free_all
commands.description: Queries the amount of free memory.

commands.name: memory_load_list
commands.description: Loads a sweep list from a named file in memory into the current list.

commands.name: memory_store_list
commands.description: Stores the current sweep list to a named file in memory.

commands.name: mmemory_catalog
commands.description: Outputs a catalog (list) of files for a given mass-storage unit specifier (msus).

commands.name: mmemory_data
commands.description: Loads or retrieves a file as an IEEE 488.2 definite-length block from mass memory.

commands.name: mmemory_copy
commands.description: Copies a file in mass memory to a new name.

commands.name: mmemory_delete_name
commands.description: Deletes a named file from mass memory (optionally specifying the msus).

commands.name: mmemory_move
commands.description: Renames or moves a file in mass memory.

commands.name: mmemory_load_list
commands.description: Loads a sweep list from a named file in mass memory.

commands.name: mmemory_store_list
commands.description: Stores the current sweep list to a named file in mass memory.

commands.name: system_preset
commands.description: Returns the signal generator to the user-defined or factory preset state (the front-panel Preset key).

commands.name: system_preset_all
commands.description: Resets all states, including persistent states, to their factory defaults.

commands.name: system_preset_type
commands.description: Selects whether Preset returns the instrument to the NORMal (factory) or USER preset state.

commands.name: system_preset_save
commands.description: Saves the current instrument state as the user preset state.

commands.name: system_error_next
commands.description: Reads and removes the oldest entry from the error queue, returning the error number and message.

commands.name: system_idn
commands.description: Sets or queries a user-definable identification string returned by *IDN?.

commands.name: system_opt
commands.description: Sets or queries a user-definable option string returned by *OPT?.

commands.name: system_version
commands.description: Queries the SCPI version with which the instrument complies.

commands.name: system_date
commands.description: Sets or queries the instrument real-time clock date.

commands.name: system_time
commands.description: Sets or queries the instrument real-time clock time.

commands.name: system_language
commands.description: Selects the remote command language/personality (e.g. SCPI or legacy compatibility modes).

commands.name: system_communicate_gpib_address
commands.description: Sets or queries the GPIB (HP-IB) address of the signal generator.

commands.name: system_communicate_lan_ip
commands.description: Sets or queries the LAN IP address.

commands.name: system_communicate_lan_hostname
commands.description: Sets or queries the LAN hostname.

commands.name: system_communicate_gtlocal
commands.description: Returns the signal generator to local (front-panel) control.

commands.name: status_operation_event
commands.description: Queries (and clears) the standard operation status event register.

commands.name: status_operation_condition
commands.description: Queries the standard operation status condition register.

commands.name: status_operation_enable
commands.description: Sets or queries the standard operation status enable register.

commands.name: status_questionable_event
commands.description: Queries (and clears) the questionable status event register.

commands.name: status_questionable_condition
commands.description: Queries the questionable status condition register.

commands.name: status_questionable_enable
commands.description: Sets or queries the questionable status enable register.

commands.name: status_preset
commands.description: Presets the status register enable masks and transition filters to their default values.
